rationale: Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz is a major qualitative escalation beyond ongoing US-Iran strikes — it directly threatens global oil supply, which would cause significant energy price spikes affecting Poland's economy and daily costs.

top_events.title: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz after US strikes
top_events.summary: Iran has announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz following continued US military strikes — a major escalation beyond the ongoing exchange of strikes. Roughly 20% of global oil and LNG passes through this chokepoint; closure would cause severe energy price spikes directly hitting Polish fuel, heating and electricity costs.

top_events.title: US-Iran strikes continue for second consecutive day
top_events.summary: US and Iran exchanged strikes across the Middle East for a second day in a row, with three Indian sailors missing after a tanker was hit in the Gulf of Oman. The conflict is escalating in intensity and geographic scope, raising energy security and regional stability risks that indirectly affect Poland.

top_events.title: Ukraine strikes military plant deep inside Russia
top_events.summary: Ukraine conducted long-range missile strikes hitting a military plant deep inside Russian territory. This is a continuation of Ukraine's deep-strike campaign with no fundamental change in the conflict's character — ongoing background risk for Poland.
